title: Pod disk-usage bar in Model Library (volume + ephemeral pods)
description: Surface the connected Pod's disk usage as a bar in the Model Library, under the 'N installed · M available' sub-line and over the filters — so the user can manage disk space while browsing/installing models (not only in RunPod Settings). Also make the bar work for EPHEMERAL 'Any region' pods, which use the container disk (/cubric-data) not a network volume. Today the bar only exists in Settings (MPI-169) and only for volume-backed pods. Two gaps: (1) Model Library has no bar; (2) ephemeral pods report no usable telemetry — the wrapper's /wrapper/disk du's /workspace (CUBRIC_VOLUME_MOUNT default), but ephemeral models live in /cubric-data (start.sh sets CUBRIC_ROOT=/cubric-data but never exports CUBRIC_VOLUME_MOUNT), so du reads the wrong, unused ~20GB mount. Fix: start.sh exports CUBRIC_VOLUME_MOUNT=$CUBRIC_ROOT (R2-floated runtime, NO image rebuild); server /remote/pod/disk returns {used,total,ephemeral} resolving total from volume size OR ephemeral container-disk size; extract the Settings bar's mount+poll+colour-swap into ONE shared helper both surfaces call (no drift); Model Library mounts it; Settings reuses it and gains the ephemeral case for free.
